D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Landmark Colour Cues — Way-Finding & Maps

Coloured landmarks improve route recall ~30%; London Underground line colours (Beck 1933) and hospital corridor colour codes are textbook applications

Episodic memory uses distinct landmarks as anchor points. Coloured landmarks compete less with adjacent context — easier to retrieve direction-of-turn. Map design principle: use ≤ 7 distinguishable colours (working memory limit), prefer category-specific hues (water = blue universally).

Add coloured landmarks to a city block grid.
